text.title: Test Fixtures Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end example: Factory + Object Mother + transactional rollback + xdist worker DB.

example.code:
import uuid import factory from factory.django import DjangoModelFactory from myapp.models import User
class UserFactory(DjangoModelFactory): class Meta: model = User
email = factory.LazyFunction(lambda: f"u-{uuid.uuid4()}@example.com") name = factory.Faker("name") role = "user"

example.note: Object Mother on top of Factory (r-mother-pick) — semantic test intent.

example.code: @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def django_db_setup(worker_id, django_test_environment, django_db_blocker): from django.conf import settings db_name = "test_mydb" if worker_id == "master" else f"test_mydb_{worker_id}" settings.DATABASES["default"]["NAME"] = db_name with django_db_blocker.unblock(): from django.test.utils import setup_databases setup_databases(verbosity=0, interactive=False)

example.note: Bad — God Fixture with Sequence under xdist (r4, r5 violations).
